question: Describe the primary function of the Control Unit (CU) within the CPU and explain how it directs the operation of the other main hardware components.
expectedPoints: The Control Unit (CU) is the component of the CPU that directs the operation of the processor.
It interprets instructions from the program and converts them into a series of control signals that activate other parts of the computer.
It coordinates the activities of the ALU, registers, and input/output devices to execute the instruction fetch-decode-execute cycle.

question: List the five classic components of a computer according to the von Neumann architecture and provide a one-sentence description of each component's primary role.
expectedPoints: Input Unit: Accepts data and instructions from the outside world.
Output Unit: Sends processed results to the outside world.
Memory Unit: Stores data and instructions.
Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU): Performs arithmetic and logical operations on data.
Control Unit (CU): Directs the operation of all other components.

question: Explain the complete fetch-decode-execute cycle, detailing the role of the Program Counter (PC), Memory Address Register (MAR), Memory Data Register (MDR), and Instruction Register (IR) in the process.
expectedPoints: Fetch: The address of the next instruction is copied from the PC to the MAR; the instruction is fetched from memory and placed into the MDR, then copied to the IR; the PC is incremented.
Decode: The control unit decodes the instruction in the IR to determine the operation to be performed and the operands required.
Execute: The control unit generates the control signals to perform the operation, which may involve the ALU, accessing data from memory, or performing I/O.
The cycle then repeats, fetching the next instruction from the updated PC.
